structured.intro: Iran's seizure of **two major container vessels—EPAMINONDAS and MSC FRANCESCA—on 2026-04-22** in the Strait of Hormuz, coupled with concurrent attacks on **EUPHORIA**, has triggered a critical breakdown in Gulf transit confidence: **vessel traffic has collapsed 92% to 9 transits/day from a 110/day baseline**, while **war risk premiums have become unquotable for VLCC/LR2 tonnage** (last reference 5.2% hull value) and **AG–East VLCC rates have spiked to $164k/day—a 5.8x jump from $28k baseline**. The suspension of major carriers **including Maersk, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM**, combined with **15 dark vessel detections in 24 hours** and the emergence of US naval interdiction (TOUSKA seizure on 2026-04-20), signals an escalating dual-blockade scenario that is rapidly rendering conventional Strait routing economically and operationally untenable for containerized and tanker cargoes.
